title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
slides: Tidal blocks AI-made tracks from earning revenue. Why now?
Major labels had already flagged thousands of suspicious uploads.
Tidal uses metadata checks—not audio analysis—to stop payouts.
If your AI track was monetized, expect an unpaid hold.
Audit your catalog now before Tidal removes all payouts.
thread_text:
1. Revenue frozen.

Tidal has stopped payments on all AI-generated tracks, even ones already monetized.

2. Labels flagged it first.

Universal, Sony, and Warner sent Tidal lists of thousands of suspicious uploads before the policy change.

3. Not listening—just checking.

Tidal blocks payouts based on metadata flags, not audio analysis or listening.

4. Your payout is on hold.

If an AI track you uploaded was earning, Tidal will now hold that revenue unpaid indefinitely.

5. Audit your catalog now.

Find every track with suspicious metadata and remove it before Tidal pulls all future payouts.
video_script: Tidal stops paying for AI tracks.

script: Tidal stops paying for AI tracks.

instagram.caption:
Revenue frozen on all AI-generated tracks at Tidal—even if they were already earning cash.

Major labels like Universal, Sony, and Warner flagged thousands of suspicious uploads before Tidal acted.  
Tidal isn't listening to the audio; it blocks payouts by scanning metadata alone.  
If you uploaded an AI track that was paid out, that revenue is now on indefinite hold.

You must audit your catalog now and remove any AI-made track with suspicious metadata, or Tidal will strip all future payouts.

Who in your network uploaded AI music without realizing the payout risk? 🚫💰🔍

Tidal blocks AI track payouts based on metadata flags, not audio analysis or listening. Fast, automated enforcement.
